summary: Acoustic gems and electric fire; Bird Song & Althea shine
blurb: The show opens with a pristine acoustic set featuring Bird Song, Ripple and It Must Have Been The Roses. The second half erupts into high‑energy electric numbers like Althea, Jack Straw and Deal, capped by the classic China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue. Overall performance is strong, especially Jerry’s vocals and guitar.
review: On September 25, 1980 at the Warfield, the Dead delivered a two‑sided marvel that still feels fresh for listeners today. The first set rolls out an acoustic treasure trove—Jerry’s soaring Bird Song, the sweet melancholy of Ripple, and heartfelt renditions of It Must Have Been The Roses and Jack‑A‑Roe—all captured in crystal‑clear soundboard fidelity that lets every nuance shine despite a brief mic hiccup. When the band switches to electric for Set 2, the energy spikes; Althea glides into a raucous Jack Straw, Deal charges forward with relentless drive, and the beloved China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider pair lands with unmistakable punch. The third set builds a soaring jam from Playing In The Band through Drums, Space, and an emotive Wharf Rat before landing on a joyous Good Lovin', while the encore U.S. Blues rounds it out with that trademark grin. For fans seeking the definitive listening experience, the acoustic soundboard recording is the clear winner—its pristine clarity lets you hear every vocal inflection and guitar tone, making this show a must‑listen for both acoustic‑era collectors and anyone craving a night where the Dead were truly locked in.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings (acoustic SBD and unknown good) are flagged as positive, praising the acoustic set’s clarity and overall performance; one audience tape is mixed but still notes strong vocals and electric highlights. The preponderance of positive language across multiple sources justifies a moderate positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of the acoustic set with superb fidelity; minimal mic issues and overall pristine audio make it the definitive listening source.

key_highlights: Pristine acoustic set featuring Bird Song, Ripple, It Must Have Been The Roses and Jack‑A‑Roe
Electric surge with Althea, Jack Straw, Deal and the classic China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue
Third‑set jam from Playing In The Band through Space into Wharf Rat ending on an uplifting Good Lovin'
Soundboard recording offers crystal‑clear audio, while even the fair audience tape conveys high energy
song_highlights: I've Been All Around This World
It Must Have Been The Roses

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers soaring vocals and a warm guitar tone; minor mic static noted but does not detract from his strong performance.

must_listen_sequences: I've Been All Around This World
It Must Have Been The Roses
Oh Babe, It Ain't No Lie
